Title: User Account Inventory Review
Description: Review of all user, administrator, and service accounts to confirm they are authorised, accurately recorded, and assigned to active personnel.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: exported account list from identity provider or directory service, showing account name, type, owner, and last login date. Analysis: cross-reference against HR active employee list; identify accounts with no login in over 45 days, accounts with no documented owner, and service accounts without a recorded business purpose. Conclusion: document findings, flag exceptions, confirm remediation actions. Conduct review at minimum quarterly.

Title: Privileged Access Rights Review
Description: Periodic review of all privileged and administrative accounts to confirm that elevated access remains necessary and is not excessive.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: list of all accounts with privileged or administrative rights, including role justification and approver. Analysis: verify each privileged account has a documented business justification and was approved by an authorised manager; confirm no standard user accounts have accumulated privileged rights; confirm privileged accounts are not used for general day-to-day activities. Conclusion: document and remediate unjustified access. Conduct review at minimum every six months.

Title: Account Provisioning and Deprovisioning Process Review
Description: Verification that access is granted following a documented approval process and revoked promptly upon termination or role change.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: sample of access provisioning tickets (last period), corresponding HR records confirming role and start date; sample of offboarding records and corresponding access revocation confirmations. Analysis: confirm each provisioned account has documented manager approval; confirm all terminated employees had access revoked on or before last working day; confirm role changes triggered access adjustments. Conclusion: document gaps and timelines. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Multi-Factor Authentication Coverage Review
Description: Verification that MFA is enforced on all externally exposed applications, remote access, and administrative interfaces.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: list of externally accessible systems and administrative interfaces; MFA configuration screenshots or export from identity provider. Analysis: confirm MFA is enabled and enforced (not optional) for all external-facing systems, remote network access, and admin consoles; document any exceptions with compensating controls. Conclusion: document MFA coverage rate and any gaps. Conduct review at minimum annually.

Title: Role-Based Access Control Review
Description: Review of role definitions and assigned permissions to confirm they reflect current business requirements and enforce least privilege.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: role matrix or RBAC configuration export showing roles, permissions, and assigned users; business justification documentation per role. Analysis: confirm each role has a documented purpose and owner; confirm no role grants more access than required for its business function; confirm users are assigned only roles appropriate to their job function. Conclusion: document roles requiring remediation. Conduct review annually or upon significant organisational change.

Title: Segregation of Duties Verification
Description: Verification that conflicting duties are separated so no single individual can perform incompatible actions without detection.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: list of critical processes where SoD applies (e.g. financial approvals, access provisioning, code deployment); current role assignments for personnel involved in those processes. Analysis: confirm no individual holds roles that allow both initiation and approval of the same transaction or action; flag and document any exceptions with compensating controls. Conclusion: document SoD violations and remediation plans. Conduct review annually.

Title: Default Account and Password Management Review
Description: Verification that default vendor accounts have been renamed or disabled and default credentials changed across all systems.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: system hardening checklist or configuration scan output; list of known default accounts per system type. Analysis: confirm all default accounts are disabled or renamed; confirm no default credentials remain active; confirm this check is performed as part of system commissioning. Conclusion: document any systems with unremediated defaults. Conduct review at minimum annually or upon new system deployment.

Title: Hardware Asset Inventory Review
Description: Periodic verification that the hardware asset inventory is complete, accurate, and includes all devices connected to the network.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: current asset inventory export; output from active discovery tool or DHCP logs; network scan results. Analysis: reconcile discovered devices against inventory; identify unauthorised or unrecorded devices; confirm each asset has an assigned owner and classification. Conclusion: document discrepancies and initiate investigation for unknown devices. Conduct review at minimum quarterly.

Title: Software Inventory and Authorisation Review
Description: Verification that only authorised and currently supported software is installed across enterprise assets.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: software inventory report from endpoint management tool or manual collection; list of authorised software titles. Analysis: identify software not on the authorised list; confirm all authorised software is currently supported by the vendor (no end-of-life); document exceptions where unsupported software must remain with a risk acceptance record. Conclusion: initiate removal of unauthorised software; document exceptions. Conduct review at minimum monthly.

Title: Data Inventory and Flow Review
Description: Review of the data inventory and documented data flows to confirm sensitive data is identified, classified, and flows are current.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: data inventory or data register; data flow diagrams; output of any DLP tool showing sensitive data locations. Analysis: confirm the data inventory covers all known sensitive data repositories; confirm data flows reflect actual data movement between systems and third parties; identify any undocumented flows or new data types. Conclusion: update inventory and flows; document gaps. Conduct review annually or upon significant system or process change.

Title: Internal Vulnerability Scan
Description: Automated scanning of internal systems to identify known vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: vulnerability scan report from authenticated internal scan, including scan date, scope, tool, and findings by severity. Analysis: confirm scan covered all in-scope internal systems; verify all critical and high findings have remediation owners and target dates; confirm prior critical findings have been resolved or have documented risk acceptances. Conclusion: record pass/fail per criticality threshold. Conduct at minimum quarterly.

Title: External Vulnerability Scan
Description: Automated scanning of externally exposed systems and services to identify vulnerabilities accessible from the internet.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: external vulnerability scan report including scan date, scope, and findings by severity. Analysis: confirm scan covered all externally reachable IP addresses and services; verify all critical and high findings have remediation owners and deadlines; confirm prior critical findings are resolved. Conclusion: record pass/fail per criticality threshold. Conduct at minimum monthly.

Title: Patch Deployment Review
Description: Verification that operating system and application patches are being applied within required timeframes across all in-scope systems.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: patch compliance report from endpoint management or patch management tool, showing patch status by system and age of outstanding patches. Analysis: confirm critical patches are deployed within defined SLA (typically 30 days); confirm no systems have unpatched critical vulnerabilities beyond SLA without a documented exception; confirm patch deployment follows the change management process. Conclusion: document non-compliant systems and escalation actions. Conduct review monthly.

Title: Penetration Test Execution and Finding Remediation
Description: Commissioning and tracking of periodic penetration tests, and verification that findings are remediated within defined timeframes.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: penetration test report (current period) including scope, methodology, tester credentials, and findings; remediation tracker showing status of all findings from current and prior test. Analysis: confirm test was conducted by a qualified independent party; confirm scope covered both network and application layers; confirm all critical and high findings have remediation plans with owners and dates; confirm prior critical findings are resolved before the next test. Conclusion: document outstanding findings and escalate overdue items. Conduct at minimum annually.

Title: Vulnerability Remediation Tracking
Description: Ongoing tracking of open vulnerabilities to ensure remediation progresses within risk-based timeframes.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: vulnerability remediation tracker or ticketing system export showing open items, severity, assigned owner, and age. Analysis: confirm all critical vulnerabilities have remediation in progress or a documented risk acceptance; confirm remediation SLAs are being met; identify and escalate overdue items. Conclusion: produce remediation compliance rate; escalate critical aged items. Conduct review monthly.

Title: Log Collection and Configuration Review
Description: Verification that logging is enabled, correctly configured, and centralised across all in-scope systems.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: logging configuration export or screenshot from SIEM/log management platform showing sources, event types, and time synchronisation status; list of in-scope systems. Analysis: confirm all in-scope systems are forwarding logs to the centralised platform; confirm event types cover authentication, privilege use, configuration change, and data access; confirm time sync is active on all sources. Conclusion: document unconfigured or missing sources. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Log Review and Anomaly Investigation
Description: Periodic review of security event logs and SIEM alerts to detect anomalous activity and potential incidents.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: log review records or SIEM alert disposition log for the review period, showing alerts reviewed, analyst actions, and outcomes. Analysis: confirm all critical and high alerts were reviewed and dispositioned within SLA; confirm at least weekly manual review was performed; confirm anomalous events were escalated as incidents where appropriate. Conclusion: document unreviewed alerts and missed SLAs. Conduct review weekly at minimum; critical alerts reviewed within defined SLA (typically 24 hours).

Title: Log Retention Compliance Review
Description: Verification that logs are retained for the required minimum period and are protected from tampering.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: log retention configuration from SIEM or log management platform; storage capacity report; access control list for log storage. Analysis: confirm retention period meets the defined minimum (typically 12 months, 90 days online); confirm log storage has adequate capacity and has not silently dropped logs; confirm write access to logs is restricted to authorised service accounts only. Conclusion: document retention gaps. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Service Provider Log Collection Review
Description: Verification that logs from key cloud and third-party service providers are collected and integrated into centralised monitoring.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: list of in-scope service providers; SIEM configuration showing service provider log sources; sample of service provider logs ingested. Analysis: confirm all critical service providers have log forwarding configured; confirm authentication, admin activity, and data access events are captured; confirm service provider logs are subject to the same retention policy as internal logs. Conclusion: document missing service provider log sources. Conduct review at minimum annually.

Title: Incident Response Plan Review and Test
Description: Annual review of the incident response plan and execution of a tabletop exercise or simulation to test response capability.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: current incident response plan document with version and review date; tabletop exercise or simulation report showing scenario, participants, outcomes, and action items. Analysis: confirm the plan has been reviewed and updated within the last 12 months; confirm a test was conducted involving key stakeholders; confirm action items from the test have been tracked and closed. Conclusion: document plan gaps and outstanding action items. Conduct at minimum annually.

Title: Post-Incident Review Execution
Description: Structured review following every significant security incident to identify root cause, lessons learned, and control improvements.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: post-incident review report for the incident including timeline, root cause analysis, control gaps identified, and action items with owners and due dates. Analysis: confirm review was conducted within the defined timeframe post-closure (typically 2 weeks); confirm root cause was identified; confirm action items have been assigned and are being tracked; confirm lessons learned have been fed back into policy or control updates where applicable. Conclusion: document outstanding action items. Conduct following every significant incident.

Title: Security Incident Reporting Channel Review
Description: Verification that the incident reporting mechanism is documented, communicated, and accessible to all personnel.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: documentation of reporting channels (email, helpdesk, hotline); evidence of communication to staff (intranet post, training record, email); test of reporting channel (e.g. submission of test report and confirmation of receipt). Analysis: confirm channel is accessible to all staff including remote workers; confirm response and triage SLA is defined and being met; confirm reporting instructions are included in security awareness training. Conclusion: document gaps. Conduct review annually.

Title: Change Request Approval and Documentation Review
Description: Verification that all production changes were submitted via the formal change process, approved, tested, and documented.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: change log for the review period; sample of change tickets (minimum 10 or all if fewer) showing request, approver, test evidence, and outcome. Analysis: confirm no changes were made without a corresponding approved ticket; confirm emergency changes were reviewed post-implementation; confirm rollback plans were defined for significant changes. Conclusion: document unapproved or undocumented changes. Conduct review monthly.

Title: Security Awareness Training Completion Review
Description: Verification that all personnel have completed mandatory annual security awareness training and acknowledged security policies.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: training platform completion report showing completion status per employee, date completed, and training version; acknowledgement records for the security policy. Analysis: confirm 100% completion rate for employees in scope; identify overdue personnel and escalation actions taken; confirm training content was reviewed and updated within the last 12 months. Conclusion: document non-completion rate and follow-up actions. Conduct review at minimum annually.
Policy Procedure: Human_Resources_Security > Security Training Delivery and Tracking

Title: Pre-Employment Background Screening Review
Description: Verification that background screening is consistently conducted for new hires with access to sensitive systems or data.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: sample of new hire onboarding records from the review period; corresponding background screening confirmation records. Analysis: confirm screening was initiated and completed before or at the point of access provisioning for each sampled hire; confirm screening scope is proportionate to access level; confirm screening results are documented and retained. Conclusion: document cases where access was provisioned before screening was completed. Conduct review annually.

Title: Offboarding Access Revocation Review
Description: Verification that all system access is revoked on the last day of employment for all departing personnel.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: sample of offboarding records from the review period; corresponding access revocation confirmations from identity provider and key systems. Analysis: confirm access was revoked on or before the last working day for all sampled cases; confirm physical access badges were collected; confirm no dormant accounts remain active for departed employees beyond 45 days. Conclusion: document cases with delayed revocation. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Cryptographic Key and Certificate Lifecycle Review
Description: Verification that cryptographic keys and TLS certificates are managed per policy, rotated on schedule, and that no weak algorithms are in use.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: key management register showing all active keys with algorithm, length, creation date, and next rotation date; certificate inventory with expiry dates; configuration scan or manual check for deprecated algorithms. Analysis: confirm no keys are overdue for rotation; confirm no TLS 1.0 or 1.1 is enabled; confirm no deprecated algorithms (DES, RC4, MD5 for signing, SHA-1 for signing) are in use; confirm no certificates expire within 30 days without a renewal in progress. Conclusion: document overdue rotations and impending expiries. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Firewall Ruleset Review
Description: Periodic review of firewall and network security device rules to confirm all rules are justified, current, and consistent with the approved architecture.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: current firewall ruleset export; network segmentation diagram; list of approved rules with documented business justification. Analysis: confirm each rule has a documented owner and business purpose; identify and flag rules that are overly permissive, unused, or undocumented; confirm CDE or sensitive zone isolation rules are intact. Conclusion: document rules to be removed or tightened. Conduct review at minimum every six months.

Title: Network Segmentation Verification
Description: Verification that network segmentation controls are in place and effective, preventing unauthorised lateral movement between zones.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: network architecture diagram; firewall or VLAN configuration showing zone separation; results of segmentation test (manual traffic test or scan output). Analysis: confirm sensitive or regulated zones are isolated from general network; confirm there are no uncontrolled paths between zones; confirm wireless networks are segregated from wired internal networks. Conclusion: document any unintended paths or segmentation failures. Conduct review annually or after significant network changes.

Title: Remote Access Security Review
Description: Verification that all remote access connections use approved encrypted channels with MFA enforced, and that inactive sessions are terminated.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: VPN or remote access platform configuration showing MFA enforcement and session timeout settings; access logs showing remote sessions for the review period. Analysis: confirm MFA is enforced for all remote access; confirm split tunnelling is disabled or has documented approval; confirm session timeout is configured; confirm all remote access is via approved encrypted channels (no legacy protocols). Conclusion: document exceptions. Conduct review at minimum annually.

Title: Network Architecture and Diagram Review
Description: Verification that network architecture documentation is current and accurately reflects the deployed environment.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: current network architecture diagram(s); change log for the review period. Analysis: confirm the diagram was updated following all significant network changes in the period; confirm diagram shows all major zones, boundaries, and security controls; confirm the diagram is version controlled and stored in an approved location. Conclusion: document diagram inaccuracies. Conduct review annually or after significant changes.

Title: Physical Access Rights Review
Description: Periodic review of physical access rights to restricted areas to confirm access remains appropriate and terminated employees have no remaining access.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: physical access control system export showing active badges and access rights per zone; HR termination records for the review period. Analysis: confirm no terminated employee retains active physical access; confirm access to restricted areas is limited to personnel with documented business need; confirm visitor access procedures are being followed. Conclusion: document unjustified access rights. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Clear Desk and Clear Screen Compliance Check
Description: Spot-check verification that personnel are following clear desk and clear screen practices to prevent unauthorised access to sensitive information.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: completed clear desk inspection checklist (physical walkthrough) covering workstations, meeting rooms, and shared areas; or screenshot evidence of screen lock activation after inactivity timeout. Analysis: confirm workstations auto-lock within defined timeout; confirm no sensitive documents or credentials are left unattended; confirm printer output areas are checked. Conclusion: document non-compliant findings and corrective actions taken. Conduct spot checks at minimum quarterly.

Title: Media Disposal Verification
Description: Verification that storage media containing sensitive data is disposed of using approved destruction methods and that destruction is documented.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: media destruction log or certificate of destruction for the period; list of assets decommissioned in the period. Analysis: confirm all decommissioned storage media had sensitive data confirmed and destruction method documented; confirm destruction method is appropriate to data classification; confirm certificates of destruction are retained where applicable. Conclusion: document any disposals without evidence of secure destruction. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Supplier Security Assessment Review
Description: Periodic review of the security posture of critical and high-risk suppliers against defined security requirements.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: supplier risk register with tier classification; evidence of security assessment per supplier (questionnaire responses, SOC 2 reports, ISO certificates, or equivalent) for the review period. Analysis: confirm all critical/high-risk suppliers were assessed within the defined review period; confirm findings from prior assessments have remediation plans; confirm new suppliers were assessed before onboarding. Conclusion: document overdue assessments and unresolved findings. Conduct review annually for critical suppliers, as defined by risk tier.

Title: Supplier Contract Security Requirements Review
Description: Verification that contracts with in-scope suppliers include required security clauses and that obligations are current.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: sample of supplier contracts for in-scope suppliers; contract review checklist covering security requirements (data protection, incident notification, right to audit, subprocessors). Analysis: confirm required security clauses are present in each sampled contract; confirm contracts have been reviewed since the last significant change to security requirements; flag contracts due for renewal that should be updated. Conclusion: document contracts with missing or outdated clauses. Conduct review annually.

Title: Supplier Offboarding and Data Return Verification
Description: Verification that supplier access is fully revoked and organisational data is returned or destroyed when a supplier relationship ends.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: list of supplier relationships terminated in the review period; corresponding offboarding records showing access revocation confirmation, data destruction or return confirmation, and any certificates of destruction. Analysis: confirm all access was revoked on or before the contract end date; confirm data return or destruction was confirmed in writing; confirm the supplier has been removed from the active supplier register. Conclusion: document incomplete offboardings. Review within 30 days of each termination.

Title: Backup Execution and Restore Test
Description: Verification that scheduled backups are completing successfully and that data can be restored within defined recovery objectives.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: backup job completion report for the period showing job status, size, and any failures; restore test record showing data restored, test date, time to restore, and comparison against RPO. Analysis: confirm no backup jobs have failed silently; confirm a restore test was performed for a representative sample of critical systems; confirm restore time meets defined RTO. Conclusion: document backup failures and restore test results. Conduct backup review weekly; restore tests at minimum quarterly.

Title: BCP and DR Plan Test
Description: Periodic test of business continuity and disaster recovery plans to verify that critical systems can be recovered within defined objectives.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: test plan and scenario description; test execution record with participant list, outcomes per recovery objective, and identified gaps; post-test action log. Analysis: confirm the test covered critical systems and recovery objectives (RTO/RPO); confirm all action items from the test have been assigned owners and dates; confirm the plan was updated following the test. Conclusion: document gap between tested recovery time and defined RTO. Conduct at minimum annually.
Policy Procedure: Business_Continuity > BCP and DR Plan Review

Title: Data Classification and Labelling Compliance Review
Description: Verification that information assets are classified in accordance with the classification scheme and that labelling is applied where required.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: sample of documents and data stores created or modified in the review period; classification labels or metadata tags applied; access controls in place per classification level. Analysis: confirm sampled items have a classification label; confirm access controls are appropriate to the classification; confirm any sensitive data shared externally was authorised and transmitted via approved channels. Conclusion: document unlabelled or misclassified items. Conduct review at minimum annually.

Title: Data Retention and Disposal Compliance Review
Description: Verification that data is retained for required minimum periods and securely disposed of at the end of the retention period.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: retention schedule; list of data disposals performed in the period with destruction method and classification; automated retention policy configuration where applicable. Analysis: confirm disposals match the retention schedule; confirm destruction method is appropriate to data classification; confirm certificates of destruction are retained for sensitive data. Conclusion: document data retained beyond its schedule and overdue disposals. Conduct review annually.

Title: Threat Intelligence Review
Description: Periodic review of threat intelligence sources to identify emerging threats relevant to the organisation and update risk assessments accordingly.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: threat intelligence source list (subscriptions, feeds, ISACs); summary of intelligence reviewed in the period; record of any risk register updates triggered by intelligence findings. Analysis: confirm at least one reliable threat intelligence source is actively monitored; confirm intelligence findings are reviewed at defined intervals; confirm material new threats are reflected in the risk register or have a documented rationale for not being escalated. Conclusion: document intelligence gaps and actions taken. Conduct review at minimum monthly.

Title: Legal and Regulatory Compliance Review
Description: Periodic review of applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual information security requirements to confirm they remain current and are being met.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: register of applicable legal and regulatory requirements with review date; evidence of compliance activities per requirement (e.g. audit reports, certifications, legal counsel sign-off). Analysis: confirm the register was reviewed within the last 12 months; confirm any new or changed requirements have been assessed for impact; confirm compliance activities are documented. Conclusion: document requirements with no evidence of compliance activity. Conduct review annually or when significant regulatory changes occur.

Title: Independent Information Security Review
Description: Periodic independent review of the information security programme, including controls, policies, and management processes.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: independent review or audit report (internal audit, external auditor, or qualified third party); scope and methodology documentation; management response to findings. Analysis: confirm the reviewer is independent of the function being reviewed; confirm review scope covered key policies, controls, and management processes; confirm all findings have management responses and remediation plans. Conclusion: document outstanding findings. Conduct at minimum annually.

Title: Internal Information Security Compliance Review
Description: Periodic self-assessment of compliance with internal information security policies, procedures, and standards.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: compliance review checklist or assessment report covering key policies and procedures; list of non-compliances identified and corresponding corrective actions. Analysis: confirm review covered all major policy areas; confirm non-compliances have assigned owners and target remediation dates; confirm findings from prior reviews have been resolved. Conclusion: document compliance rate and outstanding corrective actions. Conduct review at minimum annually.

Title: Security Policy Violation Management Review
Description: Verification that the disciplinary process for security policy violations is documented, applied consistently, and that violations are tracked.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: policy violation register for the period; sample of cases showing investigation record, outcome, and disciplinary action applied; evidence that the disciplinary process is communicated to all staff. Analysis: confirm all reported violations were logged and investigated; confirm outcomes are proportionate and consistent; confirm the process is referenced in the employee handbook or security policy acknowledgement. Conclusion: document uninvestigated reports or inconsistent outcomes. Conduct review annually.
Policy Procedure: Human_Resources_Security > Security Training Delivery and Tracking

Title: Endpoint Configuration Baseline Review
Description: Verification that all endpoint devices are configured against the approved hardened baseline and that deviations are detected and remediated.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: approved hardened configuration baseline document; configuration compliance scan output from endpoint management tool showing compliance rate per device or device group. Analysis: confirm all in-scope endpoints have been assessed against the baseline; identify devices with non-compliant settings; confirm critical deviations have remediation actions in progress; confirm baseline was reviewed within the last 12 months. Conclusion: document compliance rate and outstanding deviations. Conduct review at minimum quarterly.

Title: Malware Protection Review
Description: Verification that anti-malware and endpoint detection tools are deployed, current, and operating on all in-scope devices.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: endpoint protection platform coverage report showing deployment status, signature update date, and scan status per device; list of in-scope devices. Analysis: confirm anti-malware is deployed on all in-scope endpoints; confirm signatures were updated within the last 24 hours; confirm no devices have had protection disabled without authorisation; confirm behaviour-based detection is enabled where supported. Conclusion: document unprotected or out-of-date devices. Conduct review monthly.

Title: Web and Email Filtering Review
Description: Verification that web content filtering and email security controls are active, current, and effectively blocking malicious content.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: web filtering platform configuration and block statistics for the period; email security gateway configuration showing DMARC, SPF, DKIM, and anti-malware settings; sample of blocked event logs. Analysis: confirm web filtering is applied to all users including remote workers; confirm DNS filtering is active; confirm email DMARC policy is enforced; confirm anti-malware scanning is active on email attachments. Conclusion: document misconfigured or bypassed controls. Conduct review quarterly.
Policy Procedure: Endpoint_Security > Web and Email Filtering Review

Title: Software Installation Control Review
Description: Verification that controls preventing unauthorised software installation are active and effective on all endpoint devices.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: application control or allowlisting configuration from endpoint management platform; log of software installation attempts (authorised and blocked) for the period. Analysis: confirm application allowlisting or equivalent control is active on all in-scope endpoints; confirm unauthorised installation attempts are being blocked and logged; confirm the authorised software list was reviewed within the last six months. Conclusion: document devices without application control and any unauthorised software found. Conduct review at minimum semi-annually.

Title: Endpoint Encryption Compliance Review
Description: Verification that full-disk encryption is enabled on all endpoint devices that store or access sensitive data.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: endpoint encryption compliance report from endpoint management or MDM platform showing encryption status per device. Analysis: confirm full-disk encryption is enabled on all laptops, desktops, and mobile devices that process sensitive data; confirm encryption key management is in place; confirm any unencrypted devices have a documented exception with compensating controls. Conclusion: document unencrypted devices and exceptions. Conduct review quarterly.

Title: Data Loss Prevention Review
Description: Verification that DLP controls are active and configured to detect and prevent unauthorised transmission of sensitive data.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: DLP platform policy configuration; DLP incident report for the period showing events detected, severity, and disposition. Analysis: confirm DLP policies cover defined sensitive data types; confirm DLP is applied to email, web upload, and removable media channels; confirm DLP incidents were reviewed and responded to; confirm false positive rate is within acceptable thresholds. Conclusion: document control gaps and unreviewed incidents. Conduct review quarterly.
Policy Procedure: Endpoint_Security > Web and Email Filtering Review

Title: Secure Development Lifecycle Compliance Review
Description: Verification that security activities are embedded in the software development lifecycle including requirements, design, coding, testing, and deployment.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: SDL policy or process documentation; sample of development projects from the period showing security activities at each phase (security requirements documented, threat model or security review completed, SAST/DAST results, security sign-off before deployment). Analysis: confirm security requirements are defined for all projects handling sensitive data; confirm threat modelling or security design review is conducted for significant changes; confirm no code was deployed to production without security testing results reviewed. Conclusion: document projects with missing security activities. Conduct review at minimum annually.

Title: Static and Dynamic Application Security Testing Review
Description: Verification that automated security testing (SAST/DAST) is integrated into the development pipeline and findings are tracked to resolution.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: SAST and/or DAST tool scan reports for the review period; findings tracker showing open issues, severity, and remediation status. Analysis: confirm SAST is run on all code commits or at minimum on each release; confirm DAST is performed on all production applications at minimum annually; confirm all critical and high findings have remediation owners and target dates; confirm no critical findings are deployed to production without documented risk acceptance. Conclusion: document applications without scan coverage. Conduct review at minimum quarterly.

Title: Secure Coding Standards Compliance Review
Description: Verification that developers are following approved secure coding standards and that code reviews include a security component.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: secure coding standards document with review date; sample of code review records showing security checklist completion; developer training completion records. Analysis: confirm secure coding standards are documented and accessible to all developers; confirm code reviews include a security element (checklist or reviewer qualification); confirm all developers with production code access completed role-specific security training in the last 12 months. Conclusion: document projects with no security code review and untrained developers. Conduct review annually.

Title: Application Security Requirements Review
Description: Verification that information security requirements are defined and approved for all new or significantly changed applications before development begins.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: sample of project initiation records or design documents for new/changed applications in the period; evidence of security requirements review or sign-off. Analysis: confirm security requirements are documented for each sampled project; confirm requirements were reviewed by Information Security before development commenced; confirm requirements address authentication, authorisation, data protection, and logging as applicable. Conclusion: document projects started without security requirements sign-off. Conduct review at minimum semi-annually.

Title: Third-Party Software Component Review
Description: Verification that third-party libraries and open-source components used in development are inventoried, current, and free of known critical vulnerabilities.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: software bill of materials (SBOM) or dependency list per application; vulnerability scan results for third-party components (e.g. SCA tool output). Analysis: confirm all significant applications have an up-to-date dependency inventory; confirm no critical vulnerabilities exist in third-party components in production without a documented remediation plan; confirm end-of-life components have documented migration plans. Conclusion: document applications with no SBOM and unpatched critical component vulnerabilities. Conduct review monthly.

Title: Development, Test, and Production Environment Separation Review
Description: Verification that development and test environments are separated from production and that production data is not used in non-production environments without controls.
Audit Methodology: Evidence required: environment architecture diagram showing separation; access control configuration showing that production and non-production access lists are distinct; data masking or anonymisation evidence for any production data used in test. Analysis: confirm developers do not have direct production access; confirm test environments do not connect to production systems without explicit approval; confirm production data used in testing is masked or anonymised. Conclusion: document environment separation failures. Conduct review at minimum annually.
